_source: DXF migration GT — stair down/level facts (homeowner review); main floor rides ~4 risers above the garage/basement

stairs._note: garage/mudroom run down to basement, bottom at the east end. VR #44: the whole garage is sunken - this flight STARTS at the slab (top: slab) and descends to the basement from there.

VR #44 rework: the drawn 4-tread ladder IS the full flight - up from the garage slab to a small platform at main-floor level outside the mudroom door (owner markup: platform north of the treads, nothing south of them).

main stair is a switchback (VR note #14). up/down now follow the architect's own plotted text: the DXF 'DOWN' label sits on the SOUTH band (y~-256) and 'UP' on the NORTH band (y~-207), so the south band is the DOWN-flight and the north band is the UP-flight (owner: 'the plan clearly indicates up and down ... follow it'). 'split' re-reads the raw tread ladders in bbox and emits both flights so the viewer renders the landing + return flight instead of one dead-ending flat run; _switchback_bands reads _dir_labels() to assign each band's down/direction. down_near is only a fallback for wells with no nearby Up/Down text; it now points at the south (DOWN) band for consistency.

camera._note: spawn near the front entry / porch side

up_turns._note: VR#14/#58: L1 up-to-L2 is a U-turn switchback -- up, landing to the west wall, then back east up to L2

openings._note: VR#33: phantom door in the playroom closet east wall - the red arc at (-120.9,52.1) is a hall-side door symbol in wall y~57.5 that the parser force-attached to the vertical closet wall; owner: 'should just be a wall ... that's a closet'

VR#47: single front door - facade is drawn as two overlapping wall runs each carrying a 64in door; the INNER one (wall y=-279.6) becomes a cased opening so only the outer entry door reads from the yard
VR#40: laundry chute - raise the 16.6in slot to a counter-height hatch instead of a floor-to-head pass-through (proper chute+cabinet fixtures proposed separately)

palette._note: accents from owners' current house: chartreuse playroom, deep-teal sunroom (their 'reading room')
